data.description: BUILDPLAN M3 / WS-5 spike (BUILDPLAN id MA-SP-1). Question: does Barnum 0.4 expose a public standalone handler-invocation path (replacing handler.__definition.handle at processIssues.ts:61-89)? Deliverable: written verdict as a ticket comment - A (public API exists; name it, show a working call) or B (none; adapter plus exact pin plus canary required). No production code. Gates MA-11 and shapes MA-13. Size S.
data.title: Spike: Barnum public handler invocation feasibility

data.body: Verdict A — public API exists: runPipeline from @barnum/barnum/pipeline. A handler from createHandler is itself a valid single-step pipeline, so invoking one via runPipeline needs no composition. Evidence: import + call at falcon-detective/src/lib/invokeHandler.ts:1 and :39-42 (invokeHandler = async (handler, input) => runPipeline(handler, input)), consumed by processIssues.ts via the injectable InvokeHandler seam. Barnum version: package.json pins ^0.4.0; package-lock.json resolves and locks exact 0.4.0 (falcon-detective/package-lock.json:29-38, registry tarball + integrity hash). __definition grep: 'rg -F "__definition" falcon-detective/src' returns zero matches — no remaining private-API access. Caveat: node_modules/@barnum/barnum is absent in this checkout (npm-managed dep, not vendored), so this is a static-only check — source comments, the import statement, and the lockfile pin, not a live inspection of the package's type declarations/exports map. Note: retroactive verification; consumer MA-11 already merged via PR #29 (commit adf8cee) using this path.

provenance.reason: spike: no plan file needed, verdict-only

data.body: Spike deliverable verified: Verdict A comment posted (public runPipeline from @barnum/barnum/pipeline, exact-pin 0.4.0, zero __definition matches). Retroactive verification — consumer MA-11 merged via PR #29 on this path. Read-only spike: no code changes, nothing to merge.
